'Has been learns' is grammatically incorrect because 'learns' cannot follow 'has been'. The correct form is 'has been learning'.

questions.explanation: The sentence refers to an action that started in the past and will continue into the future, next week, requiring the present perfect continuous tense. The correct form is 'has been learning', as it matches the singular subject 'She' and the future context.'Has learnt' is incorrect due to its past perfect tense, not aligning with the future context. 'Have been learning' is incorrect because 'have' does not agree with the singular subject 'She'. 'Has been learns' is grammatically incorrect, as 'learns' cannot be used in this tense structure.

questions.text: What should be next in the series:7C, 10F, 14J, 19O
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Explanations will be added soon

questions.text: Complete the series. F16, H32, J48, _.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: 16 is added every time to each term and the letters alternate. So after J, K will be skipped and 48+16 is 64 so the answer is L64.

questions.text: Statement: Most development plans exist on paper only. Courses of action: I. The incharges should be instructed to supervise the fieldwork regularly. II. The supply of paper to such departments should be cut short.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If only I is the most appropriate
options.explanation: Course of Action I suggests increased supervision, which directly targets the problem of plans not being implemented practically.

options.text: If only II is the most appropriate
options.explanation: Course of Action II proposes reducing paper supply, which does not address the core issue of non-implementation and could lead to operational challenges.

options.text: If either I or II is most appropriate
options.explanation: This is incorrect because Course of Action I is the only one that effectively addresses the issue of implementation.

options.text: If neither I nor II is most appropriate
options.explanation: This is incorrect as Course of Action I provides a practical solution to the problem.

questions.explanation: The problem is a lack of implementation, not an excess of paper. Instructing supervisors to monitor fieldwork (Action I) directly addresses the gap between planning and execution. Cutting the paper supply (Action II) is an illogical and punitive measure that doesn't solve the problem.

questions.text: Statements:I. The farmers have decided against selling their Kharif crops to the government agencies.II. The government has reduced the procurement price of Kharif crops starting from last month to the next six months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the order of cause and effect is reversed. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) is the cause that led to Statement I (farmers deciding against selling their crops to the government agencies), not the other way around.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the two statements have a clear cause-and-effect relationshipThis option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) directly influences the decision made by the farmers as described in Statement I.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.

questions.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.
